channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Popular TikToker Masza Graczykowska Passes Away; Authorities Investigate Cause of Death
item.description: Masza Graczykowska, a well-known TikTok and Instagram content creator, was tragically found dead in her apartment in Warsaw, Poland on 8th March 2026. The young influencer, celebrated for her fashion ...

item.title: PPP Leader Sherry Rehman’s Daughter Marvi Malik Dies in Karachi Following Brain Haemorrhage
item.description: Karachi — Marvi Khurshid Malik, daughter of Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP) Senator and former federal minister Sherry Rehman, has passed away following a brain haemorrhage, the PPP Secretariat confirmed...

item.title: McKenzie confirms LIV Golf’s 2027 return to Steyn City amid tensions with Sunshine Tour
item.description: Sports Minister Gayton McKenzie has confirmed that the LIV Golf tournament will return to Steyn City from 22 to 25 April next year, following what he described as attempts to block this year’s event. ...

item.title: WA government urges calm amid record fuel price hikes
item.description: The Western Australian government has urged calm in the face of skyrocketing fuel prices, which have reached record highs in recent days. While the cost of fuel has surged, the state government has ru...

item.title: PM Modi surpasses Chamling to become longest-serving head of government
item.description: On Sunday, Prime Minister Narendra Modi crossed a historic milestone of serving 8,931 days in office, making him the longest-serving head of government in India. He surpassed the previous record held ...

item.title: FG Partners Coventry University to Offer UK Degrees in Nigeria
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria don start a new move wey go bring UK education come closer to home. As part of effort to reduce the popular 'japa' trend, the government don enter partnership with Co...

item.title: South Ocean Holdings Faces Profit Crisis Amidst Import Competition
item.description: South Ocean Holdings, a major electrical cable manufacturer in South Africa, is grappling with a severe profit crisis, reporting an 86% drop in operating profit for the year ending December 31, 2025. ...

item.title: CNDEN urges fair distribution of Niger Delta pipeline surveillance contracts
item.description: The Coalition of Niger Delta Ethnic Nationalities (CNDEN) don call on President Bola Tinubu make e decentralise the oil pipeline surveillance contracts to cover all the communities wey host the nation...

item.title: Tshwane explores alternative burial solutions amid shrinking cemetery space
item.description: The City of Tshwane is raising alarms over a mounting shortage of burial land, warning that existing cemetery space could be fully used up within the next 30 years. With roughly 6,000 burials needed e...

item.title: Bass Leads L.A. Mayoral Race, But Faces Significant Unfavorable Views From Voters
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass leads in the upcoming mayoral election with 25% support, but over half of voters view her unfavorably, according to a recent poll. Bass' primary challenger, City Councilme...

item.title: Meru Mother Dies in Tragic Accident as Construction Stone Hits Salon Roof
item.description: In Maua town, Meru County, a tragic accident claimed the life of 35-year-old Irene Kui G, a devoted mother of three. Irene had gone to a local salon to get her hair plaited when a large stone fell fro...

item.title: NU secures important win over FEU to maintain championship momentum
item.description: The National University (NU) Bulldogs managed to secure a hard-fought victory against the Far Eastern University (FEU) Tamaraws in a crucial UAAP volleyball match, bolstering their campaign to defend ...

item.title: CPI(M) seeks more time to discuss DMK's offer of only five seats for Tamil Nadu elections
item.description: In the lead-up to the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections scheduled for April 23, 2026, the CPI(M) has expressed dissatisfaction with the DMK's offer of just five seats as part of their seat-sharing arrange...

item.title: Supreme Court weighs limits of the notwithstanding clause in Quebec’s Bill 21 case
item.description: The Supreme Court of Canada is hearing a landmark challenge to Quebec’s Bill 21, a secularism law that bars many public‑sector workers from wearing religious symbols at work, such as hijabs and turban...

item.title: Canva maintains $60 billion valuation despite competition from Google AI
item.description: Canva’s co-founder, Cliff Obrecht, has confirmed that the company’s private valuation of $60 billion remains steady, despite growing concern among investors following the launch of Google’s AI-powered...

item.title: Sammy Gyamfi Leads Ghana’s Effort to Centralize Gold Trading and Boost Exports
item.description: One year after Ghana launched its bold plan to centralise gold trading, Sammy Gyamfi is at the forefront of efforts to formalise the country’s most important export. Known for his past as a combative ...

item.title: Governor Alia Says Only Benue People Go Decide 2027 Election Based on Performance
item.description: Benue State Governor, Hyacinth Alia, don talk say no single person or 'emperor' get power to determine who go win election for Benue come 2027. The governor give this warning through him Chief Press S...

item.title: South Africa’s New Labour Reforms Could Improve Rights for Gig Economy Workers
item.description: South Africa’s Minister of Employment and Labour is working on major changes to key labour laws, including the Basic Conditions of Employment Act and the National Minimum Wage Act, to extend better pr...

item.title: Investigation into Seatbelt Use After Fatal Sydney Crash
item.description: A fatal crash in Western Sydney on Sunday has prompted an investigation into how seatbelts were used by the passengers involved. A Subaru travelling on Stuart Road, Dharruk, near Mount Druitt, left th...

item.title: ENHYPEN Pop-up Store Opens in the Philippines for Fans
item.description: Fans of K-pop group ENHYPEN in the Philippines now have the chance to buy merchandise directly made by the artists themselves. ABS-CBN reports that a pop-up store featuring ENHYPEN-made items has open...
